MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION On The Second Cause Of Action
Off Calendar Feb-02-2023 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF ALLIED WORLD NATIONAL ASSURANCE COMPANY's Motion for Summary Adjudication On The Second Cause Of Action.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Allied World National Assurance Co.'s motion for summary adjudication of its complaint's second cause of action is denied.

The "complaint for declaratory relief" regards insurance coverage. In its complaint's first cause of action - as to which summary adjudication is not sought - "Allied World seeks a judicial determination that there is no coverage under the Allied World Umbrella Policy." (Id. at 11:18-20.) In the second cause of action - as to which summary adjudication is sought - "Allied World seeks a judicial determination" that, if it is eventually found to owe insurance coverage, "all other insurance" must first be exhausted. (Id. at 12:17-20, 12:1-5.) Thus, with the first cause of action yet to be adjudicated, Allied World improperly seeks an advisory opinion as to the second cause.

Under California's declaratory relief statutes: "The court may refuse to exercise the power granted by this chapter in any case where its declaration or determination is not necessary or proper at the time under all the circumstances." (CCP 1061; Otay Land Co. v. Royal Indemnity Co. (2008) 169 Cal.App.4th 556, 563 ["The courts do not issue advisory opinions about the rights and duties of the parties under particular agreements, if no actual, justiciable controversy has yet developed."].)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Declaratory Relief Complaint Of Plaintiff; **Court Reporter Fees Paid**
Off Calendar Mar-11-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Randall P. Berdan, Esq. (415.745.3770; rberdan@nicolaidesllp.com) and Ian A. Cooper, Esq. (312.585.1400; icooper@nicolaidesllp.com) of Nicolaides Fink Thorpe Michaelides Sullivan LLP for Plaintiff, Allied World National Assurance Company.
Stephen Wong, Esq. (415.352.6200; swong@spcclaw.com) of Sinnott, Puebla, Campagne & Curet, APLC for Defendant, Zurich American Insurance Company.
Aaron Gruber, Esq. (650.458.4040; agruber@rallsgruber.com) of Ralls Gruber & Niece LLP for Defendants, Skanska USA Civil West California District Inc. and Skanska USA Building Inc. Pamela A. Bower, Esq. (408.341.0234; pbower@hbalawgroup.com) of Hess Bower Adams-Hess, PC for Defendant, The Herrick Corporation.
Barron Weinstein, Esq. (415.927.6920; bweinstein@mwncov.com) of Weinstein & Numbers, LLP for Defendant, Transbay Joint Powers Authority.
Brookes Degen, Esq. (415.954.4400; bdegen@fbm.com) of Farella Braun + Martel LLP for Defendant, Webcor-Obayashi Joint Venture.

A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Defendant The Herrick Corporation's motion to stay the declaratory relief complaint filed by plaintiff Allied World National Assurance Company is granted in part. The first cause of action seeking a declaration whether the Allied policy provides indemnity coverage for the underlying lawsuits is stayed until the conclusion of the underlying lawsuits or further court order except that any party may bring a summary judgment motion based solely on undisputed facts in the complaints in the underlying actions and the policy to establish that the Allied policy does or does not provide any indemnity coverage for some or all of the claims in the underlying lawsuits. The second and third causes of action seeking declarations regarding priority of indemnity coverage as between Allied's policy and the Zurich Builders Risk Policy and the Zurich Building Policy is stayed until the conclusion of the underlying lawsuits or further court order except that any party may bring a summary judgment motion based solely on the language of the policies and undisputed extrinsic evidence bearing on the interpretation of the policies to establish the priority of the policies. Permitting Allied's declaratory relief claims to proceed based on undisputed facts and language of the policies, without allowing any discovery in this case, is fully consistent with Montrose and its progeny. Permitting any other litigation on those claims runs afoul of Montrose and its progeny.

Order is signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HEK)

Off Calendar Mar-11-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Randall P. Berdan, Esq. (415.745.3770; rberdan@nicolaidesllp.com) and Ian A. Cooper, Esq. (312.585.1400; icooper@nicolaidesllp.com) of Nicolaides Fink Thorpe Michaelides Sullivan LLP for Plaintiff, Allied World National Assurance Company.
Stephen Wong, Esq. (415.352.6200; swong@spcclaw.com) of Sinnott, Puebla, Campagne & Curet, APLC for Defendant, Zurich American Insurance Company.
Aaron Gruber, Esq. (650.458.4040; agruber@rallsgruber.com) of Ralls Gruber & Niece LLP for Defendants, Skanska USA Civil West California District Inc. and Skanska USA Building Inc. Pamela A. Bower, Esq. (408.341.0234; pbower@hbalawgroup.com) of Hess Bower Adams-Hess, PC for Defendant, The Herrick Corporation.
Barron Weinstein, Esq. (415.927.6920; bweinstein@mwncov.com) of Weinstein & Numbers, LLP for Defendant, Transbay Joint Powers Authority.
Brookes Degen, Esq. (415.954.4400; bdegen@fbm.com) of Farella Braun + Martel LLP for Defendant, Webcor-Obayashi Joint Venture.

A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Defendant The Herrick Corporation's demurrer to all three causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiff Allied World National Assurance Company is off calendar in light of the ruling on Herrick's alternative motion to stay. Relying on Montrose Chemical Corp. v. Superior Court (1993) 6 Cal. 4th 287 and its progeny, Herrick makes essentially identical arguments on its demurrer as it does on its motion to stay. However, Montrose states that the remedy for a conflict between the interests of an insurer and insured with respect to coverage and underlying actions is a stay, not the sustaining of a demurrer. Thus, Herrick's arguments based on Montrose are addressed in ruling on Herrick's motion to stay and not Herrick's demurrer.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HEK)

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Randall P. Berdan, Esq. (415.745.3770; rberdan@nicolaidesllp.com) and Ian A. Cooper, Esq. (312.585.1400; icooper@nicolaidesllp.com) of Nicolaides Fink Thorpe Michaelides Sullivan LLP for Plaintiff, Allied World National Assurance Company.

Aaron Gruber, Esq. (650.458.4040; agruber@rallsgruber.com) of Ralls Gruber & Niece LLP for Defendants, Skanska USA Civil West California District Inc. and Skanska USA Building Inc. Pamela A. Bower, Esq. (408.341.0234; pbower@hbalawgroup.com) of Hess Bower Adams-Hess, PC for Defendant, The Herrick Corporation.
Barron Weinstein, Esq. (415.927.6920; bweinstein@mwncov.com) of Weinstein & Numbers, LLP for Defendant, Transbay Joint Powers Authority.
Brookes Degen, Esq. (415.954.4400; bdegen@fbm.com) of Farella Braun + Martel LLP for Defendant, Webcor-Obayashi Joint Venture.

A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Defendant Skanska USA Building Inc.'s demurrer to the third cause of action in the complaint filed by plaintiff Allied World National Assurance Company is sustained without leave to amend. Allied does not allege, nor does it appear that it can allege, that it has a contractual relationship or any other relationship with Skanska Building for which a declaration is available per CCP 1060. The fact that Skanska Building is being defended under the Arch policy, a policy as to which Allied does not allege any dispute, is insufficient to support a legally cognizable dispute between Allied and Skanska Building regarding the priority of indemnity coverage as between Allied's policy and the Zurich Building Policy.

Order is signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HEK)

Off Calendar Mar-11-2022 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Randall P. Berdan, Esq. (415.745.3770; rberdan@nicolaidesllp.com) and Ian A. Cooper, Esq. (312.585.1400; icooper@nicolaidesllp.com) of Nicolaides Fink Thorpe Michaelides Sullivan LLP for Plaintiff, Allied World National Assurance Company.
Stephen Wong, Esq. (415.352.6200; swong@spcclaw.com) of Sinnott, Puebla, Campagne & Curet, APLC for Defendant, Zurich American Insurance Company.
Aaron Gruber, Esq. (650.458.4040; agruber@rallsgruber.com) of Ralls Gruber & Niece LLP for Defendants, Skanska USA Civil West California District Inc. and Skanska USA Building Inc. Pamela A. Bower, Esq. (408.341.0234; pbower@hbalawgroup.com) of Hess Bower Adams-Hess, PC for Defendant, The Herrick Corporation.
Barron Weinstein, Esq. (415.927.6920; bweinstein@mwncov.com) of Weinstein & Numbers, LLP for Defendant, Transbay Joint Powers Authority.
Brookes Degen, Esq. (415.954.4400; bdegen@fbm.com) of Farella Braun + Martel LLP for Defendant, Webcor-Obayashi Joint Venture.

A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Defendant Zurich American Insurance Company's demurrer to all three causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiff Allied World National Assurance Company is overruled as to all three causes of action. The disputes whether the Allied policy provides indemnity coverage for the underlying lawsuits and the priority of indemnity coverage as between Allied's policy and the Zurich Builders Risk Policy and the Zurich Building Policy are sufficiently concrete to make declaratory relief appropriate. Moreover, Allied has sufficiently alleged that it needs a declaration to help it to determine how it should proceed with regard to the underlying lawsuits and that it would suffer hardship without the declarations that it seeks.

Order is signed.
